rationale: This page provides specific historical and cultural details about the Śaiva Manuscripts of Pondicherry, including their inscription date, volume, thematic scope, and historical spread. This is high-quality content for generating factual questions.
cleanedText:
Śaiva Manuscripts of Pondicherry (Successfully inscribed in 2005)
• The largest collection of Śaiva Siddhanta texts, consisting of 11,000 manuscripts on Shaivism, Vishnu worship, astrology, medicine, and literature.
• In the 10th century CE, this religious tradition, a major current of Hinduism, was spread right across the Indian subcontinent and beyond, as far as Cambodia in the East.
• It represented the mainstream of Tantric doctrine and Shaiva worship.
• Its surviving texts, the majority of them unpublished, range from the 6th century CE to the colonial period.
